blockingIssues.description: Atomic init cleanup is incomplete: artifacts are tracked only after each creation call returns (persistIdentity, persistKey, openEncryptedDb). If a multi-file write function (e.g., persistIdentity which writes both identity.json and identity.key) fails partway through, partially written files are not cleaned up because they haven't been registered in createdArtifacts yet. Fix: register anticipated artifact paths BEFORE calling the creation function, or have creation functions return the paths they wrote for cleanup registration.

isValidOptionalId accepts undefined in addition to null, but the MessageEnvelope contract types nullable fields as `string | null` (not `string | null | undefined`). This means runtime validation via validateEnvelope will silently accept envelopes with missing/undefined fields (in_reply_to, dedupe_key, trace_id) that should be rejected as structurally invalid. Fix: isValidOptionalId should only accept null (not undefined) for nullable envelope fields, or add an explicit undefined check in validateEnvelope.

nonBlockingIssues.description: ID validation (isValidId) only checks for non-empty strings but does not enforce the documented typed prefixes (msg_, thr_, trc_, dup_) that the module comments describe as contract semantics. This may lead to accepting malformed IDs from external input in future CLI commands.

appliedUpdates.description: Added SQLCipher in-memory keying caveat to .factory/library/environment.md: better-sqlite3-multiple-ciphers does not support keying on :memory: DBs, so preflight/tests must use temp on-disk files.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update mors-worker skill to use Vitest-compatible test filtering syntax (e.g., `npx vitest run test/init.test.ts`) instead of Jest-style `--testPathPattern` flags that don't work with this project's Vitest setup.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Features core-init-identity-and-atomic-bootstrap and core-envelope-and-state-contracts both had workers encounter failures when using `npm run test -- --testPathPattern <pattern>` as documented in skill/feature verification steps. Workers had to independently discover the Vitest-compatible alternative.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a documented exception or clarification in the mors-worker skill for initial scaffold/bootstrap features where strict red-first TDD (write failing test before any code) is impractical because the test harness itself is being established.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Features core-project-scaffold-and-quality-gates and core-init-identity-and-atomic-bootstrap both showed transcripts where implementation code was written before test files, deviating from the skill's strict TDD guidance. This is inherent to scaffold work where the test framework doesn't exist yet.

rejectedObservations.observation: Feature verification instructions in features.json also use Jest-style test filtering
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate - this is the same root cause as the skill test-filtering observation and would be addressed by the same fix
